theme: Every corner narrates — the office should tell its own story without a single word

ideas.title: Wall Clock with Vibe-Reactive Hands
ideas.description: Place a small SVG analog clock on the upper-left wall (left: 5%, top: 8%), showing actual local time with sweeping second hand. The clock face is vibe-aware: 'crushing' = hands spin 2x faster than real time (optical illusion of urgency); 'slow-day' = second hand drags with a rubber-band ease, hesitates each tick; 'on-fire' = clock face tints red, hands stutter; 'after-hours' = clock dims to 30% opacity with a crescent moon replacing the center dot. Implemented as a pure SVG with `transform: rotate()` driven by `Date.now()` in a `useEffect` interval. WHY: Time is the invisible spine of a workday. A living clock anchors every vibe state in temporal reality — it answers 'how long has this chaos been going on?' at a glance, and it makes the upper-left dead zone earn its real estate.

ideas.title: Worker Dossier Drawer on Click
ideas.description: The `onWorkerClick` prop already threads through Office.jsx → Character.jsx → App.jsx, but from the UI nothing visible happens when you click a worker. Implement a slide-up panel (bottom 35% of viewport, z-index above floor): large CharacterAvatar, worker name + role badge, current status with human-readable duration (e.g., 'Working for 47 min'), task description, and their last 3 activity log entries as a mini timeline. Dismiss on click-outside or Escape. Props needed are all on the worker object + activityEntries already passed to Office. WHY: The office floor rewards visual curiosity with zero information. People click workers expecting something — right now they get nothing. The dossier is the payoff that turns clicking into a habit.

ideas.title: Away Sign Floats Above Idle Desks
ideas.description: When a worker's status is not 'working' and they haven't updated in 10+ minutes, render a tiny sticky-note SVG (12×14px, yellow, slight rotation) floating above their desk monitor. The note text is pulled from a small array seeded by worker ID + Math.floor(idleMinutes / 10), cycling through: '☕ Coffee run', '🧠 Deep think', '📞 On a call', '🚶 Taking a lap', '🥪 Lunch', '🎧 Headphones in'. The note fades in with a 300ms CSS transition and disappears when status returns to working. WHY: Idle desks currently look abandoned — they lose their narrative presence. Away signs maintain the fiction that everyone is still HERE, just momentarily human. It costs zero new data and closes the 'where did Sofia go?' question instantly.

ideas.title: Today's Vibe Ribbon on Dashboard
ideas.description: Below the Team Vibe indicator on the Dashboard tab, render a 24-slot horizontal ribbon — one slot per hour of the current day (0h–23h). Each slot is a 12×20px rounded rectangle colored by dominant vibe during that hour: purple (crushing), orange (on-fire), teal (in-flow), grey (slow-day), indigo (after-hours), or dark (no data). Store vibe-change events in a `useRef` history array inside App.jsx — whenever getTeamVibe output changes, push `{ vibe, timestamp }`. Derive slot colors from this in-memory log, no API needed. Hover a slot: show a tooltip '2pm · In Flow'. WHY: The vibe badge answers NOW. The ribbon answers TODAY'S ARC — 'was it chaotic this morning before it calmed down?' That's the narrative question every observer actually has, and right now there's no way to answer it.

ideas.title: Printer as Physical Incident Monitor
ideas.description: The bottom-left corner (left: 3%, top: 75%) currently has a tiny decorative plant. Relocate the plant one grid cell right and place a SVG printer (40×50px) in that corner. States: (1) READY — small green LED dot, label 'READY'; (2) PRINTING — LED blinks, paper feed animation (a white rectangle slides up from paper tray every 3s when a worker is 'working'); (3) JAMMED — triggers when any worker hits 'error' status: printer shakes (CSS keyframe), LED turns orange, crumpled paper emoji pokes out from tray. (4) CLEARED — when error resolves, printer plays a 'job complete' slip animation (green paper ejects and fades). All state derived from workers prop — no new data. WHY: Errors are invisible until you scroll the log. A physical comedic incident indicator turns system state into environmental storytelling. It's the feature that makes people say 'wait, the printer actually responds to errors?'

ideas.title: Coffee Corner Reacts to Team Energy
ideas.description: The coffee corner already has `data-vibe={vibe}` on its wrapper — but nothing in the CSS actually changes per-vibe. Add 5 CSS rules to Office.css: 'crushing' = steam animation plays fast (2 stacked ::before/::after pseudo-elements drifting upward) + '3 ORDERS' badge; 'slow-day' = emoji switches to 🥶 cold cup via a data-attribute on the emoji span + no steam; 'on-fire' = steam turns red-orange, label becomes 'DECAF??'; 'after-hours' = emoji becomes 🌙, opacity drops to 40%, label 'CLOSED'. This is almost entirely CSS — the vibe attribute is already there, just untapped. WHY: The coffee corner is literally a vibe-aware element that currently looks the same in every state. Closing this loop takes maybe 20 CSS lines and it's the most effortless environmental upgrade available.

ideas.title: Progress Ring Replaces Status Dot on Dashboard Cards
ideas.description: Each Dashboard worker card currently shows a status-colored dot (●) next to the status label. Replace this dot with a 28px SVG progress ring: a circle with stroke-dashoffset calculated from the worker's `progress` field (0–100). Color matches role color (WORKER_ROLE_COLORS already defined in App.jsx). During 'working': ring fills clockwise up to progress %. During 'error': ring pulses red with a 1s animation. During 'idle': ring shows progress frozen with reduced opacity. WHY: 'Working' tells you state; a ring tells you HOW FAR. At a glance you see who's 12% in vs 88% done vs just started, and the Dashboard becomes spatially scannable rather than a text read.

ideas.title: The Intercom — Bloberto Broadcasts Live
ideas.description: Add a small SVG intercom/speaker unit on the left wall just above the whiteboard (left: 3%, top: 45%). Once per session (and whenever vibe changes), the intercom activates: a 'pulsing ring' animation plays on the unit for 2 seconds, then a speech bubble emerges with a procedurally-generated all-hands message built from live data: e.g., '📢 ALL HANDS: Team is 5 strong. Maya's 87% done. Carlos has been heads-down for 2h. Someone check on Dave. This is not a drill.' Message is assembled from workers array + activityEntries with a small sentence template library (~12 templates, no AI). The intercom glyph stays on-wall as a persistent fixture; click it anytime to replay the last broadcast. WHY: The whiteboard has a static vibe-phrase; the intercom creates a NARRATIVE EVENT — something happened, Bloberto noticed, he addressed the floor. It rewards return visitors ('oh something new') and makes the office feel occupied by an actual manager.
